name: Nitroglycerin
description: A volatile vasodilator which relieves angina pectoris by stimulating guanylate cyclase and lowering cytosolic calcium. [PubChem]
toxicity: Increased intracranial pressure, with any or all of persistent throbbing headache, confusion, and moderate fever; Vertigo; Palpitations; Visual disturbances; Nausea and vomiting (possibly with colic and even bloody diarrhea); Syncope (especially in the upright posture); Air hunger and dyspnea, later followed by reduced ventilatory effort; Diaphoresis, with the skin either flushed or cold and clammy; Heart block and bradycardia; Paralysis; Coma; Seizures; Death.
mechanismOfAction: Similar to other nitrites and organic nitrates, nitroglycerin is converted to nitric oxide (NO), an active intermediate compound which activates the enzyme guanylate cyclase. This stimulates the synthesis of cyclic guanosine 3',5'-monophosphate (cGMP) which then activates a series of protein kinase-dependent phosphorylations in the smooth muscle cells, eventually resulting in the dephosphorylation of the myosin light chain of the smooth muscle fiber. The subsequent release of calcium ions results in the relaxation of the smooth muscle cells and vasodilation.
pharmacodynamics: Nitroglycerin, an organic nitrate, is available in many forms as a vasodilator. Nitroglycerin is used in the treatement of angina pectoris and perioperative hypertension, to produce controlled hypotension during surgical procedures, to treat hypertensive emergencies, and to treat congestive heart failure associated with myocardial infarction.

interactions.name: Nicorandil
interactions.description: Nicorandil may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Ethanol
interactions.description: Ethanol may increase the vasodilatory activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Alteplase
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Alteplase can be decreased when it is combined with Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Methohexital
interactions.description: Methohexital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Primidone
interactions.description: Primidone may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Phenobarbital
interactions.description: Phenobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Pentobarbital
interactions.description: Pentobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Secobarbital
interactions.description: Secobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Thiopental
interactions.description: Thiopental may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Methylphenobarbital
interactions.description: Methylphenobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Thiamylal
interactions.description: Thiamylal may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Amobarbital
interactions.description: Amobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Hexobarbital
interactions.description: Hexobarbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Barbital
interactions.description: Barbital may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Barbexaclone
interactions.description: Barbexaclone may increase the hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Duloxetine
interactions.description: Nitroglycerin may increase the orthostatic hypotensive activities of Duloxetine.

interactions.name: Levodopa
interactions.description: Nitroglycerin may increase the orthostatic hypotensive activities of Levodopa.

interactions.name: Risperidone
interactions.description: Nitroglycerin may increase the hypotensive activities of Risperidone.

interactions.name: Dapoxetine
interactions.description: Dapoxetine may increase the orthostatic hypotensive activities of Nitroglycerin.

interactions.name: Heparin
interactions.description: Nitroglycerin may decrease the anticoagulant activities of Heparin.

interactions.name: Rosiglitazone
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Nitroglycerin is combined with Rosiglitazone.
